title: Wildcraft offers the best value for money in the luggage market.
body: Wildcraft has the highest value score (36.1) compared to other brands, indicating that customers perceive its products as offering the best balance of quality and price. This is likely due to its strong positioning as a marketplace brand, which allows it to offer high-quality products at competitive prices.

title: Skybags has a higher average rating than Safari and AmericanTourister.
body: Skybags has an average rating of 4.23, which is higher than Safari (4.03) and AmericanTourister (4.13). This suggests that customers are generally more satisfied with Skybags' products, which may be due to its focus on smooth wheels and handle quality, two key features that customers value highly.

title: AmericanTourister has the highest average discount, indicating a more aggressive pricing strategy.
body: AmericanTourister has the highest average discount (34.3%), which is significantly higher than the other brands. This suggests that AmericanTourister is taking a more aggressive pricing strategy to attract customers, which may be a key factor in its positioning as a marketplace brand.

title: Sentiment analysis reveals that customers are highly satisfied with Wildcraft and AmericanTourister.
body: The sentiment analysis shows that Wildcraft (81.3%) and AmericanTourister (83.2%) have the highest sentiment scores, indicating that customers are highly satisfied with their products. This suggests that these brands are doing a good job of meeting customer needs and expectations, which is a key factor in driving customer loyalty and retention.

title: Safari has a lower value score compared to other brands, indicating a potential quality-price mismatch.
body: Safari has a lower value score (17.83) compared to other brands, indicating that customers may perceive its products as being overpriced relative to their quality. This may be due to Safari's positioning as a marketplace brand, which may not be as effective in communicating value to customers as other brands.
